one_line_takeaway: SDD Planner is a diffusion-based, style-conditioned trajectory planner for autonomous driving (evaluated on StyleDrive/NuPlan against imitation and rule-based baselines) with no security, adversarial, or attacker-facing content -- it is off-topic for an AI-security knowledge graph.
key_finding: On StyleDrive, SDD Planner achieves the top SM-PDMS score of 84.23, a 3.9% gain over the strongest baseline (WoTE/Diffusion-Planner at ~80.2-80.3), with NC=97.81 and DAC=93.50 (0.51% and 1.22% improvements over the strongest baselines). On NuPlan, SDD Planner scores 91.83 on Val14, 91.76 on Test14, and 80.32 on Test14-hard, ranking first on all three splits and edging out PLUTO (90.68/91.29/80.12 respectively); versus PLUTO on Val14 the paper reports 6.05% fewer collisions and 3.15% higher TTC, plus a perfect Progress score of 100.0. Ablations show attention design matters substantially (Fixed/uniform attention: Coll.=6.50, TTC=8.20 vs. proposed learnable distance-sensitive attention: Coll.=1.85, TTC=3.24, further improved to Coll.=1.70 with cost-based selection), and full-module ablation drops NuPlan Total Score from 91.83 (full model) to 85.01 (fixed attention), 80.70 (fixed guidance), and 77.89 (both removed). The distilled/quantized deployment variant on NVIDIA Orin achieves end-to-end latency of 93.0 +/- 3.0 ms (breakdown: 4.5ms preprocessing, 14.0ms style-aware encoder, 66.5ms DDIM-15 denoising, 8.0ms selection/output), operating at ~10-11 Hz.
core_contribution: The paper proposes SDD Planner (Stylish Diffusion Drive Planner), a diffusion-based trajectory planning framework for autonomous driving that reconciles safety constraints with personalized driving styles (aggressive/normal/conservative). It has two main components: (1) a Multi-Source Style-Aware Encoder that fuses ego-vehicle state, dynamic agents, static map, and traffic-light context using a learnable distance-sensitive attention bias (Mdist = -kappa * Ddist) combined with temporal self-attention (governed by gamma) and spatial self-attention, fused via a Kronecker product into a stylized conditioning representation z_style; and (2) a Style-Guided Dynamic Trajectory Generator built on a classifier-guided DDPM whose score function is modified by a time-step-adaptive energy guidance term lambda(t)=0.3+1.2*t/T, fusing a collision-avoidance energy and a speed-compliance energy with dynamically weighted priorities (w_collision, w_speed), followed by feasibility-oriented post-processing and cost-based trajectory selection (J_sel = eta_col*C_col + eta_spd*C_spd + eta_comf*C_comf).
threat_model: Not applicable. This is a robotics/autonomous-driving motion-planning paper with no adversary, attacker capability, or security threat model. 'Safety' here refers to collision avoidance and traffic-rule compliance in normal driving conditions, not adversarial robustness, and 'risk' refers to physical/traffic risk (e.g., obstacle proximity, relative speed, road curvature), not an attack surface.
limitations: The paper's own text notes several caveats rather than a security review would: (1) hyper-parameter kappa transferred directly from StyleDrive to NuPlan without recalibration causes 'moderate degradation' (Table IV: Coll. rises from 1.85 to 2.65, TTC from 3.24 to 4.10), indicating limited cross-domain generalization of the learned distance-attenuation coefficient without tuning. (2) The real-vehicle deployment evaluation is explicitly qualitative/anecdotal -- only two representative scenarios (conservative obstacle avoidance, aggressive lane merging) are shown, with no quantitative closed-loop statistics, collision counts, or statistical significance reported for the physical deployment, and the paper itself calls this 'preliminary real-world executability.' (3) No adversarial, out-of-distribution stress-testing, or safety-critical failure-mode analysis is presented; robustness analysis is limited to hyper-parameter sweeps (kappa, gamma, alpha_max, beta_max) under normal traffic conditions. (4) The paper explicitly flags LLM-based semantic priors and stronger cross-city generalization as future work, i.e., not addressed here.

quantitative_results: StyleDrive SM-PDMS: SDD Planner 84.23 vs. strongest baseline ~80.3 (WoTE 80.32 / Diffusion-Planner 80.21) -- +3.9%
StyleDrive NC: SDD Planner 97.81 vs. best baseline WoTE 97.32 (+0.51%)
StyleDrive DAC: SDD Planner 93.50 vs. best baseline WoTE 92.29 (+1.22%)
NuPlan Val14 Score: SDD Planner 91.83 vs. PDM-Closed 91.72 vs. PLUTO 90.68
NuPlan Test14 Score: SDD Planner 91.76 vs. PLUTO 91.29
NuPlan Test14-hard Score: SDD Planner 80.32 vs. PLUTO 80.12
NuPlan Val14 vs. PLUTO: collisions reduced by 6.05%, TTC improved by 3.15%, Progress = 100.0 (perfect)

Ablation (Table V, NuPlan): Uniform attention Coll.=6.50/TTC=8.20/Prog=89.00/Jerk=3.10; Static-distance Coll.=3.20/TTC=4.80/Prog=94.50/Jerk=2.15; Proposed learnable-distance Coll.=1.85/TTC=3.24/Prog=98.40/Jerk=1.42; Proposed+cost-based-selection Coll.=1.70/TTC=3.05/Prog=98.60/Jerk=1.35
Full-module ablation (Table VI, NuPlan Total Score): Full SDD 91.83; Fixed Attention (Variant 1) 85.01; Fixed Guidance weights 0.6/0.4 (Variant 2) 80.70; Full Ablation (Variant 3) 77.89
Real-vehicle Orin deployment latency: 93.0 +/- 3.0 ms total (preprocessing 4.5ms, style-aware encoder 14.0ms, DDIM-15 denoising 66.5ms, selection/output 8.0ms), ~10-11 Hz planner update rate
Optimal guidance weights identified via heatmap sweep: alpha_max = 1.2, beta_max = 2.5
